title: Bangladesh National ID Printing/Data Correction Application (Form-1) (National Identity Registration Wing)
description: The National Identity Registration Wing's (NIDW, under the Bangladesh Election Commission) "Form-1" (ফরম-১, [বিধান ৩]), "Application for correction of printing-related error(s) in the National ID Card or in the preserved data" (জাতীয় পরিচয়পত্র বা সংরক্ষিত তথ্য-উপাত্তে মুদ্রণজনিত ভুল সংশোধনের আবেদন), distributed via NIDW's own forms portal (nidw.gov.bd). Closes Bangladesh's National ID & Civic Documents vertical, its last open vertical (bringing Bangladesh to 5 of 6, alongside the already-published DMV — `bd/brta/motor-vehicle-registration-application` —, Taxes — `bd/nbr/individual-income-tax-return-form-it-11ga` —, Passport — `bd/dip/e-passport-application-form` —, and Visa — `bd/dip/machine-readable-visa-application-form` — verticals; Business Formation, via RJSC, remains Bangladesh's sole remaining open vertical). A genuine, unauthenticated, 2-page, plain print-and-fill PDF (no AcroForm widgets, confirmed via pdfjs-dist: 0 annotations across both pages), monolingual Bengali apart from the Latin acronym "NID". The embedded font's ToUnicode CMap does not resolve every glyph: a recurring subset of conjunct/complex-cluster characters (most consistently the "ত্র" cluster in "পরিচয়পত্র", "identity card/document") extracts as a NUL codepoint via both pdfjs-dist's `getTextContent()` and a node-canvas raster render (which falls back to a boxed notdef glyph for the same characters), a font-encoding defect independently confirmed this cycle rather than assumed acceptable. Every field below was nonetheless read with full confidence: the surrounding printed structure, Bengali item numbering, and the standard NID-correction vocabulary this jurisdiction already uses elsewhere in this registry (`bd/dip/e-passport-application-form`'s own citations of "জাতীয় পরিচয়পত্র"/National ID) make every gapped word unambiguous from context, and the task briefing's own independent transcription ("correction of printing errors in NID/database") corroborates the reconstructed title. Section 1 identifies the NID holder (name, NID number — always required) and, conditionally, a legal guardian (name, NID number — for a holder under 18 or a court-declared incompetent person; the form provides no boolean flag to gate this on, the same disclosed judgment call already used for this jurisdiction's own `bd/dip/e-passport-application-form` guardian block). Section 3 is a 9-row current-value/requested-value correction table (name in Bangla, name in English, father's name, mother's name, spouse's name, date of birth, address, blood group, and an "other" catch-all); the source provides no boolean gate identifying which row(s) apply to a given applicant, so every current/requested pair is modeled as unconditionally optional — an applicant is expected to complete only the row(s) relevant to their own correction, a disclosed business rule the source does not itself machine-encode. The bottom-of-page-1 signature blocks (legal guardian's, "if applicable"; applicant's, unconditional) each print a repeated "Name:" line directly under the signature/thumbprint line; consistent with this registry's own `bd/brta`/`bd/dip` precedent of not modeling a signature block's printed-name-confirmation line as a new field, these are treated as a duplicate of the identity already captured above (holderName/guardianName) and not separately modeled — only the genuinely new address/mobile/e-mail data each block also collects is modeled as new fields. Page 1's own office-only "ক্রমিক নম্বর" (Serial Number), annotated "(অফিস কর্তৃক পূরণীয়)"/"to be filled by office", and the "অংশ-ক (ফরম-১)" acknowledgement/receipt counterfoil (which duplicates the holder/guardian identity and application date already captured, plus an office-determined next-contact/issuance date) are both excluded as office-completed, not applicant-supplied. Page 2's near-identical "Urgent"/serial-number/"Signature of the responsible officer" block is likewise an office-only receipt stub and is excluded in full; only page 2's photograph attachment box is retained, as a documents[] entry. This form prints no explicit sworn-declaration/attestation sentence anywhere (unlike this jurisdiction's own `bd/brta`, `bd/dip/e-passport-application-form`, and `bd/dip/machine-readable-visa-application-form`, each of which prints a verbatim "I ... declare" statement), so no `documents[]` attestation entry is modeled here. Page 2's "Instructions for the Applicant" (6 numbered items) are informational only — not modeled as fields — but item (2)/(3) establish this schema's two identity-document requirements (a copy of the guardian's own NID, when a guardian is involved; a copy of the NID holder's own NID, always), item (4) establishes the Urgent (7 working days) vs. General (30 working days) processing-time distinction reflected in `applicationType`, and item (5) confirms this application carries no fee. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of Bangladesh, the Bangladesh Election Commission, or the National Identity Registration Wing.

authority.name: National Identity Registration Wing (Bangladesh Election Commission)

source.documentRef: "Form-1" (ফরম-১), National Identity Registration Wing, distributed via https://nidw.gov.bd/downloadForm.php, listed there as "1. Print Mistake Correction Form1.pdf."

verification.notes: GovSchema Standard Research cycle GOV-2685 (child issue GOV-2688), source independently re-scouted this same cycle under GOV-2685. Closes Bangladesh's National ID & Civic Documents vertical (5 of 6; Business Formation is the sole remaining open vertical). Independently re-fetched the source this cycle via curl (no TLS workaround needed): https://nidw.gov.bd/download/NIDForms/print_mistake_correction_form1.pdf — HTTP 200, Content-Type application/pdf, 146866 bytes, sha256 a80ee2b64d89d5a540eeac21bbe2fff0c69b28e4df1da5bdec475e8a78ef593d, Last-Modified Wed, 07 Mar 2018 04:32:02 GMT — matching the task briefing's own cited byte size exactly. Also independently re-fetched the forms index (https://nidw.gov.bd/downloadForm.php, HTTP 200, text/html, 32211 bytes), confirming the listing "1. Print Mistake Correction Form1.pdf.", and the NIDW home page (https://nidw.gov.bd/, HTTP 200, text/html), whose own page title ("Bangladesh Election Commission") and body copy (references to "NID Wing" reissuing a National Identity Card) corroborate the authority attribution. Parsed the downloaded PDF with pdfjs-dist@4.10.38 (legacy build): 2 pages, getFieldObjects() returned null (no AcroForm) and getAnnotations() returned 0 for both pages — a plain print-and-fill document, consistent with the task briefing. getTextContent() returned a complete Bengali text layer for both pages, but a recurring subset of glyphs (conjunct/complex character clusters, most consistently the "ত্র" cluster) extract as NUL (U+0000) rather than a legible Unicode character — an embedded-font ToUnicode CMap defect, not a document-corruption or fetch problem (the byte-for-byte match against the task briefing's own cited size confirms an unmodified download). This was cross-checked, not merely asserted: the PDF was additionally rasterized this cycle via pdfjs-dist + node-canvas (`page.render()` at 2.5x scale); the same clusters that produced NUL in the text layer render as a boxed "notdef" fallback glyph (occasionally showing an internal glyph-ID label) in the canvas raster, confirming the defect lives in the embedded font itself, not in a single extraction path. Every field below was nonetheless transcribed with full confidence: the printed item numbering ((ক)/(খ)/... item markers, ১/২/৩/৪ Bengali-numeral item numbers), the surrounding legible text, this jurisdiction's own established NID-correction vocabulary (this registry's `bd/dip/e-passport-application-form` already cites "জাতীয় পরিচয়পত্র"/National ID and its own gender-enum precedent for a printed-option-list-without-widget), and the task briefing's own independently-derived field list (name, NID number, father's/mother's/spouse's name, DOB, address, blood group, the field(s) being corrected, signature/attestation blocks) all corroborate the same reading, so no word was reconstructed from guesswork alone. A raw grep of the extracted text for the asterisk character (`*`) returned zero occurrences — this form prints no requiredness markup at all (like this jurisdiction's own `bd/dip/machine-readable-visa-application-form`, unlike the AcroForm-derived `bd/dip/e-passport-application-form`) — so only fields unconditionally essential to any correction request (application type/date, and the NID holder's own name/NID number) are marked `required: true`; the guardian block, every row of the Section-3 correction table, and the guardian's own signature-block contact fields are left optional, and the applicant's own signature-block contact fields (address, mobile number) are marked required as the unconditionally-necessary means of reaching the applicant about the outcome, per Instruction (4)'s own "shall inform the applicant" clause — all disclosed judgment calls consistent with this registry's own `bd/brta`/`th/dlt`/`bd/dip` precedent for source forms without printed requiredness markup. `bloodGroup` is modeled as a plain string (not enum), consistent with this registry's own `in/morth` precedent, since the source prints only a blank line with no embedded option list. Two valid conformance fixtures (0 errors each) plus 6 mutation-control fixtures (each raising exactly 1 error) are committed under conformance/bd/nidw/print-mistake-correction-form-1/1.0.0/, checked against a from-scratch scratch ajv validator built directly from this schema.json's own fields[]/documents[] (this registry does not yet ship a committed conformance-fixture runner — see spec/proposals/0016-conformance-fixtures.md). See VERIFICATION.md for the full sourcing record, the complete item-to-field derivation, and every disclosed scoping/exclusion judgment call.

fields.name: applicationType
fields.label: Application Type (Urgent/General)
fields.description: The source prints a 2-option list next to unchecked boxes, not a fillable AcroForm widget, but the printed labels name an exact, exhaustive, mutually-exclusive pair, consistent with this registry's own `bd/dip` precedent (a printed option list is sufficient corroboration for enum even without a checkbox widget). Determines processing time per Instruction (4): Urgent within 7 working days, General within 30 working days.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, header row (above item 1): "আবেদনের ধরন" (Application Type) — "জরুরী" (Urgent) / "সাধারণ" (General)

fields.name: dateOfApplication
fields.label: Date of Application
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, header row: "আবেদনের তারিখ :" (Date of application) with দিন/মাস/বৎসর (day/month/year) entry boxes

fields.name: holderName
fields.label: Name (of the NID holder)
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, item ১(ক): "(ক) নাম" under "১। জাতীয় পরিচয়পত্রধারীর-" ("1. Of the National ID holder -")

fields.name: holderNidNumber
fields.label: National Identification Number (NID) (of the NID holder)
fields.description: Bangladesh NID numbers are issued in 10-, 13-, or 17-digit forms depending on the year of registration; modeled as an unconstrained string (consistent with this jurisdiction's own `bd/dip/e-passport-application-form` `nationalIdNo` precedent), bounded generously above the longest current format.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, item ১(খ): "(খ) জাতীয় পরিচিতি নম্বর (NID) :"

fields.name: guardianName
fields.label: Name (of the legal guardian, if applicable)
fields.description: Applicable when the NID holder is under 18 years of age, or has been declared legally incompetent by a court. The form provides no boolean flag to gate this field's requiredness on, the same disclosed judgment call already used for this jurisdiction's own `bd/dip/e-passport-application-form` guardian block, so this is left unconditionally optional.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, item ২(ক): "(ক) নাম" under "২। আঠারো বৎসরের কম বয়স/আদালত কর্তৃক অসামর্থ্য ঘোষিত জাতীয় পরিচয়পত্রধারীর ক্ষেত্রে, আইনানুগ অভিভাবকের-" ("2. In case of an NID holder under 18 years of age/declared legally incompetent by a court, of the legal guardian -")

fields.name: guardianNidNumber
fields.label: National Identification Number (NID) (of the legal guardian, if applicable)
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, item ২(খ): "(খ) জাতীয় পরিচিতি নম্বর (NID) :"

fields.description: Section 3 is a 9-row current-value/requested-value correction table; the source provides no boolean gate identifying which row(s) apply to a given applicant, so every current/requested pair in this table is left unconditionally optional — an applicant completes only the row(s) relevant to their own requested correction(s), a disclosed business rule the source does not itself machine-encode.

fields.description: Modeled as `date` for consistency with `requestedDateOfBirth`; per this jurisdiction's own established convention (e.g. `bd/dip/e-passport-application-form`), day/month/year entry is normalized to ISO 8601.

fields.name: applicantAddress
fields.label: Applicant's Address
fields.description: From the applicant's own signature block (right-hand block, unconditional, unlike the guardian's "if applicable" block). This and `applicantMobileNumber` are marked required as the unconditionally necessary means of reaching the applicant, consistent with Instruction (4)'s own commitment that the office "shall inform" the applicant of the outcome. The block's own repeated "Name:" line is not separately modeled; see this schema's own description for the disclosed dedup decision (it duplicates `holderName` in the ordinary case of a holder filing for themselves).
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, applicant's signature block: "ঠিকানা :" (Address:), under "আবেদনকারীর স্বাক্ষর/টিপসই" (Applicant's signature/thumbprint)

fields.name: applicantMobileNumber
fields.label: Applicant's Mobile Number
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, applicant's signature block: "মোবাইল নম্বর :" (Mobile Number:)

fields.name: applicantEmail
fields.label: Applicant's E-mail (if any)
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, applicant's signature block: "ই-মেইল (যদি থাকে):" (E-mail, if any:)

fields.name: guardianAddress
fields.label: Legal Guardian's Address (if applicable)
fields.description: From the legal guardian's own signature block (left-hand block, explicitly "if applicable"). Left unconditionally optional for the same reason as `guardianName`.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, guardian's signature block: "ঠিকানা :" (Address:), under "আইনানুগ অভিভাবকের স্বাক্ষর/টিপসই (প্রযোজ্য ক্ষেত্রে)" (Legal guardian's signature/thumbprint, if applicable)

fields.name: guardianMobileNumber
fields.label: Legal Guardian's Mobile Number (if applicable)
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, guardian's signature block: "মোবাইল নম্বর :" (Mobile Number:)

fields.name: guardianEmail
fields.label: Legal Guardian's E-mail (if any, if applicable)
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, guardian's signature block: "ই-মেইল (যদি থাকে):" (E-mail, if any:)

documents.label: Applicant's photograph
documents.sourceRef: Page 2, photograph attachment/staple box: "এখানে সংযুক্ত করুন" (Attach here) / "(স্ট্যাপল)" (Staple)

documents.label: Photocopy of the NID holder's own National ID
documents.sourceRef: Page 2, Instructions for the Applicant, item (৩): "আবেদনপত্রের সহিত জাতীয় পরিচয়পত্রধারীর জাতীয় পরিচয়পত্রের অনুলিপি জমা দিতে হইবে" ("A copy of the NID holder's own National ID must be submitted with the application")

documents.label: Photocopy of the legal guardian's own National ID (if applicable)
documents.handling: Applicable only when a legal guardian is involved (holder under 18/declared legally incompetent), per Instruction (2). The form provides no boolean flag to gate this on, the same disclosed judgment call already used for `guardianName`/`guardianNidNumber`, so this is left unconditionally optional rather than encoding a fabricated `requiredWhen` condition.
documents.sourceRef: Page 2, Instructions for the Applicant, item (২): "...আবেদনপত্রের সহিত উক্ত অভিভাবকের জাতীয় পরিচয়পত্রের অনুলিপি অবশ্যই জমা দিতে হইবে" ("...a copy of the said guardian's National ID must also be submitted with the application")

documents.label: Supporting document(s) evidencing the requested correction(s)
documents.handling: Varies by which Section-3 row(s) are being corrected (e.g. a birth certificate for a date-of-birth correction, a marriage certificate/nikahnama for a spouse-name correction); the source's own table column names this generically without enumerating a fixed document list per row, so this is modeled as a single open supporting-evidence entry rather than one entry per correction row.
documents.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 3 table, column "সংযুক্ত দলিলাদি/মন্তব্য" (Attached documents/Remarks)
